summary.intent: Address two P2 findings from Greptile's code review on PR #635, then add a parity assertion test suggested in Greptile's 5/5 summary to prevent future drift between Rust constants and shell handler PATH configuration.
summary.outcome: Three fixes landed across two commits (f5ee0d48, final push): HOME-unset fallback constant added, seed_dcg_guard_hook reworked to always log status, and a new parity assertion test pinning DCG_GUARD_EXTRA_PATH_DIRS against the handler script. dcg-guard test suite grew from 6 to 8 tests, all passing. Greptile rated PR 5/5 safe to merge.

repo: dcg-guard uses a DCG_GUARD_HOME_FALLBACK constant ("/root") to match the shell handler's ${HOME:-/root}/.local/bin fallback — both code paths must stay in sync or binary lookup silently diverges.
seed_dcg_guard_hook must call log_dcg_guard_status().await unconditionally even if create_dir_all fails, so operators always see guard state at boot.

The local E2E gate (./scripts/local-validate.sh <PR>) publishes a local/e2e=success status against a specific SHA; killing the validator mid-run leaves the gate stale and requires a fresh run against the new commit.
PR #635 is on branch warm-pond in repo moltis-org/moltis; related issue tracking the original bug is #626.
Quality gate sequence: cargo fmt → cargo +nightly clippy -p moltis-gateway --features local-llm-metal -- -D warnings → cargo test -p moltis-gateway --lib dcg_guard.

code.finding: resolve_dcg_binary had a redundant strip_prefix/format! path reconstruction dance that was replaced with a direct PathBuf::from(rel) in f5ee0d48. New test dcg_guard_extra_path_dirs_match_handler_script iterates DCG_GUARD_EXTRA_PATH_DIRS and asserts each entry appears literally in the handler script string.

workflow: Greptile bot posts PR review summaries as issue comments (not inline review comments); inline findings appear via gh api pulls/<n>/comments while summary ratings appear via gh api issues/<n>/comments.
Threading follow-up responses as top-level PR comments (gh pr comment) keeps Greptile review threads readable.
Background tasks killed with SIGTERM exit with code 144; this is expected and should be ignored when the kill was intentional.

friction: Stale local-validate.sh run published against old SHA after fixes landed, leaving the Local E2E Validation CI gate in a failed state requiring a fresh validator run.

Greptile's 5/5 summary mentioned P2 cosmetic suggestions without posting new inline comments, requiring manual cross-referencing between issue comments and pull review comments to confirm what was already fixed.
open_items: Local E2E Validation CI gate on PR #635 still needs ./scripts/local-validate.sh 635 re-run against the final commit to publish a fresh local/e2e=success status.
